signals.headline: South Korea's March 2026 annual inflation rose to 2
signals.raw: South Korea's March 2026 annual inflation rose to 2.2% YoY (from 2.0% in February), undershooting market forecasts of 2.3-2.4% due to government fuel caps offsetting oil price surges.**[1][2][3]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: YES** | **MED** confidence
signals.raw:
YES** | **MED** confidence  

This data, released April 2, 2026, by Statistics Korea, confirms contained price pressures despite Middle East-driven oil spikes, with monthly CPI at +0.3% (below 0.6% expected) and core inflation dipping to 2.2%.[1][2][3] Fuel/transport costs rose sharply (+5-10.4% YoY), but food fell to +0.5% and government measures (fuel caps, vouchers) limited the pass-through.[1]
